_comment: Adapter-creek deploy-test exclusions. Merged at runtime with Next.js's
Exclusions here are only for tests that fundamentally do not apply to
the Cloudflare Workers (workerd) deployment model — native addons,
multi-worker forwarding, or other Node-only assumptions. Regressions
and flakey tests are NOT listed here.

failed: app-dir action handling should forward action request to a worker that contains the action handler (node)
app-dir action handling should forward action request to a worker that contains the action handler (edge)
app-dir action handling should not error when a forwarded action triggers a redirect (node)
app-dir action handling should not error when a forwarded action triggers a redirect (edge)
test/e2e/app-dir/actions/app-action.test._note: Multi-worker forwarding — relies on deploying the same app to two different workers (one with the action handler, one without) so the request-receiving worker can forward a serialized Next-Action to the handler worker over a separate fetch. Creek deploys each app as a single worker; the fixture's two /delayed-action/(node|edge) routes collapse into the same isolate and the 'forwarded' request never carries a valid Next-Action body. Skipping rather than red-herring debugging a model we don't support.

failed: app-dir action handling should forward action request to a worker that contains the action handler (node)
app-dir action handling should forward action request to a worker that contains the action handler (edge)
app-dir action handling should not error when a forwarded action triggers a redirect (node)
app-dir action handling should not error when a forwarded action triggers a redirect (edge)
test/e2e/app-dir/actions/app-action-node._note: This file is just `require('./app-action.test')` with TEST_NODE_MIDDLEWARE=true — it re-runs the entire app-action suite under a different file path, so the same multi-worker tests re-fail here. Mirror the exclusions.

rules._exclude_note: prerender-native-module loads a native .node addon via require() in getStaticProps — workerd has no native-addon loader. async-modules exercises top-level-await inside Pages Router pages; workerd's host-initiated module evaluation returns a Promise whose `.then` throws `incompatible receiver` when awaited from adapter-owned code, and neither static-import, Promise.resolve wrapping, nor async-IIFE wrapping bridges the realm boundary. Upstream workerd limitation — revisit when workerd issues https://github.com/cloudflare/workerd/issues (TLA/module eval) land.
